data.description:
# Surface derived plan-review relationship health

## Goal
Make pin drift, enforcement posture, and review-phase compatibility understandable wherever operators inspect detailed review evidence, without adding noise to ordinary ticket lists or persisting warning events.

## Implementation
- Add the shared derived health object to detailed CLI show, MCP ticket detail, audit output, close errors, and the web ticket-review UI.
- Display each canonical related ticket, normalized role, pinned/current fingerprint status, missing-target state, enforcement effect, signed review phase, required phase, and effective execution floor as applicable.
- When pin enforcement is disabled, label observed drift advisory and keep the overall validity presentation true. Clearly distinguish legacy-unpinned from current-no-relationships.
- Keep ordinary list output and stored ticket events unchanged.
- Update stable schemas/output models and their compatibility tests.
- Document plan-material-pin, phase metadata, rollout behavior, deletion repair, and validity profiles in advanced plan-review/attestation documentation only; do not add it to basic user docs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CLI show, MCP detail, audit text/JSON, close errors, and web review UI render the same derived health semantics with canonical IDs and actionable drift reasons.
- [ ] Enforcement-off drift is visibly advisory, legacy-unpinned is distinguishable, and ordinary list output plus persisted events remain unchanged.
- [ ] Output schemas, rendering tests, and advanced documentation cover pins, phases, toggles, deleted targets, and close-versus-claim freshness.

## Verification
- Run focused audit, rendering, MCP schema, and CLI golden tests.
- Exercise the web audit page with current, drifted, disabled, legacy, and missing-target fixtures.
- Run make check and the non-integration test suite.
data.title: Expose plan-material-pin health in detailed review surfaces and advanced docs
